summary: Clear matrix mix captures a fresh European ’90 show with standout Desolation Row and Valley Road, though an abrupt cut in the Birdsong jam hurts the flow.
review: The recording is a well‑balanced matrix source that delivers crystal‑clear instrument separation and plenty of audience ambience. The band sounds energized – Jerry’s vocals are praised, Bob’s singing on Desolation Row shines, and the “Valley Road” moment is highlighted as a high point. Unfortunately an unexplained edit during the Birdsong jam detracts from an otherwise solid performance.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers rate the show 4‑5 stars and praise both the playing and sound quality; a single low‑star review notes a serious audio cut, pulling the overall impression toward mixed but still above average.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally crystal‑clear mix with good instrument balance and audience presence; however there is an unexplained cut at ~7:39 into the Birdsong jam that frustrates listeners.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set feels fresh and fun, with strong vocal moments and a lively atmosphere; the only blemish is the missing portion of the Birdsong jam.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His vocals are noted as “standing on the moon” and generally praised.
band_member_comments.Bob: Vocals on Desolation Row described as “right on”.
